hypothesis: The compiled packed AVX2 implementation reproduces the reported balance checkpoints and exact one-million-bit center prefix independently of the supplied Python implementation.
interpretation: The compiled AVX2 engine exactly matches the immutable Python run's one-million-byte SHA-256 and all reported finite discrepancy checkpoints. This validates the implementation on this finite prefix but says nothing about limiting balance.
limitations: Only one finite prefix was generated.
The timing is one low-priority run rather than a multi-repetition benchmark.
Matching implementations do not prove any infinite Rule 30 statement.

parameters.raw_encoding: one numeric byte 0x00 or 0x01 per bit

proof_scope: Exact empirical equality and counts for the finite prefix c_0 through c_999999 only.
question: problem2

software.algorithm: rule30::stream_center_prefix with packed uint64_t rows and runtime-dispatched AVX2
